message:
Fix help overlay scroll calculation to account for word-wrapped lines

The help overlay in the TUI (CassApp::render_help_overlay in src/ui/app.rs)
previously set help_content_lines based on the raw count of logical Line
objects returned by build_help_lines(). However, the Paragraph widget renders
these lines with WrapMode::Word, meaning a single logical line that is wider
than the available inner width will wrap onto multiple screen rows.

Because the scroll machinery uses help_content_lines to determine the maximum
scroll offset and to size the scrollbar thumb, using the raw logical line
count caused two visible bugs:

1. The scrollbar thumb was too large relative to the actual content height,
   since the content occupied more screen rows than the line count suggested.
2. The maximum scroll offset was too low, preventing the user from scrolling
   far enough to see all content at the bottom of the help overlay.

The fix replaces the simple `lines.len()` with a wrapped-row estimation loop
that, for each logical line, computes the display width of all its spans and
divides by the inner width (using div_ceil to round up). Empty lines are
counted as exactly 1 row. When the inner width is 1 or less (degenerate
layout), the code falls back to the raw line count to avoid division issues.

This ensures that help_content_lines accurately reflects the number of screen
rows the Paragraph will actually occupy, giving correct scrollbar sizing and
scroll limits regardless of terminal width or help text content length.

Also updates beads issue tracking metadata: the copilot connector migration
task (1u2f7.9) is now marked as in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
